items.question: Is it worth replacing a Mercedes-Benz engine?
items.answer: It depends entirely on vehicle value versus replacement cost. A GLC or W206 C-Class will almost always justify replacement — the car is worth far more than the engine. A W203 C-Class often won't; replacement cost can exceed what the car is worth outright. Use our diagnostic calculator above for a personalised verdict.

items.question: Which Mercedes-Benz engine should I avoid?
items.answer: No engine outright, but the OM651 carries a real, well-documented caveat: "Black Death" injector seal failure, worse on higher-mileage or poorly-maintained examples. It's a manageable, preventable issue with regular servicing — not a reason to avoid the engine entirely, but a reason to demand full history. The OM654 has the cleanest modern record.

items.question: What is "Black Death" on a Mercedes diesel?
items.answer: Injector seal failure that lets combustion gases leak past a degraded copper washer, forming a thick carbon deposit around the injector base. Left untreated it can weld the injector into the cylinder head, turning a £400–£1,200 seal job into a major repair. Most common on the OM646 and OM651 engines. Caught early, it's straightforward and inexpensive.

items.question: C-Class vs E-Class — which is more reliable?
items.answer: Both carry a ✅ Safe verdict in our data on their newer generations, but for different reasons. The C-Class W206 runs the more dependable OM654-only diesel range. The E-Class W213 benefits from a wider, well-supported engine range across OM654 and M276. Buy on generation and service history first, badge second.

items.question: How do I know if my Mercedes-Benz is worth repairing?
items.answer: Compare your realistic vehicle value against the full replacement quote, not just the repair estimate. As a rule, if replacement cost sits above roughly 70% of what the car is worth, get a scrap valuation before committing — the same threshold that applies across our BMW site, reflecting premium German parts and labour costs. Use our Ownership Decision Tool for a personalised assessment.

items.question: Which is the safest used Mercedes-Benz to buy?
items.answer: The GLC X254 carries the cleanest ✅ Safe verdict of any Mercedes-Benz in our data — strong values and a single, dependable diesel across the range. The W206 C-Class and W213 E-Class are close behind. See our full Mercedes-Benz Rankings for details.
